title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
short_title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
stage_label: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on National Parks. Hearings held.
plain_summary: This bill lets a special group called the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation build a monument on the National Mall in Washington DC. The monument will honor people who received the Medal of Honor, which is the highest military award for bravery. The monument must be built in a special area near famous landmarks like the Lincoln Memorial. Congress believes Medal of Honor winners deserve to be remembered in this important location because of their courage and sacrifice.
last_action_text: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on National Parks. Hearings held.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress states these facts. In two thousand twenty one, Congress and the President approved a law that lets the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation build a monument in Washington DC. This monument will honor Medal of Honor recipients and their brave actions and sacrifices. President Abraham Lincoln created the Medal of Honor one hundred sixty years ago on March twenty fifth, eighteen sixty three. President George H.W. Bush made March twenty fifth National Medal of Honor Day in nineteen ninety one. The Medal of Honor is the highest award for bravery in combat that the United States military gives to service members. Fewer Medal of Honor recipients are still alive today, which makes it more important to honor them and teach future generations about their heroism. Hershel Woody Williams was the last living World War Two Medal of Honor recipient. He died in two thousand twenty two and his body was honored at the United States Capitol. The Medal of Honor represents the best of America. It goes to ordinary people who did extraordinary things beyond what their duty required and became heroes. These examples inspire all Americans and show the spirit of our country. The Medal of Honor is important enough to our nation and history to deserve a place on the National Mall. Putting the National Medal of Honor Monument near the Lincoln Memorial would honor President Lincoln's legacy and show what regular people can do when they work for the good of everyone.

sections.title: Section 3 National Medal of Honor Monument Location
sections.text: The monument will be built in a special area. Even though federal law usually says commemorative works cannot be built in the Reserve area, this Medal of Honor monument can be built there. The Reserve is a special protected area around the National Mall as defined by federal law. All other rules about building commemorative works will still apply to this monument. These rules are found in a federal law called the Commemorative Works Act.
